data.title: IGM Financial reports record AUM of $292.6B in August 2025
data.description:
• IGM Financial (TSX:IGM:CA) reported record-high total assets under management and advisement of $292.6B, up 12.4% Y/Y and 1.6% M/M.
  • Total net inflows for August 2025 were $434M vs $142M in August 2024.
  • IG Wealth Management reached record-high assets under advisement of $151B.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Seeking Alpha News Quiz: How did Sydney Sweeney, inflation and corporate breakups affect the business world this week?
data.description:
Sydney Sweeney, inflation and corporate breakups all recently captured headlines in the word of business. Test your awareness with this week's Seeking Alpha Investment News Quiz.

data.title: Tech Voices: Tesla on xAI investment, EU vs. Google, Qualcomm on Intel
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements and remarks that could impact the technology sector.

  • Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shareholders will have a chance to vote in November on whether the EV company should invest in AI developer xAI, which is majority owned by Tesla CEO Elon Musk. 

According to a proposal appearing in Tesla's latest proxy statement, shareholders will vote on whether to authorize Tesla's board to make an investment in xAI  "in an amount and form deemed appropriate by the Board, to capitalize on the synergies between the two companies and strengthen Tesla’s position as a leader in AI, robotics, and energy."

Tesla shareholders are scheduled to vote on the measure during their annual meeting on Nov. 6.

Known largely for its AI chatbot Grok, xAI also owns X, formerly known as Twitter. SpaceX (SPACEX), another Musk company, was part of a $5 billion fundraising round for xAI announced in June, committing $2 billion. 

  • The European Commission is fining Alphabet's Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) 2.95 billion euros, or around $3.45 billion, for violating EU antitrust rules with its adtech business.

"Today’s decision shows that Google abused its dominant position in adtech harming publishers, advertisers, and consumers. This behaviour is illegal under EU antitrust rules. Google must now come forward with a serious remedy to address its conflicts of interest, and if it fails to do so, we will not hesitate to impose strong remedies," said EC official Teresa Ribera, in a statement. 

"The Commission has already signaled its preliminary view that only the divestment by Google of part of its services would address the situation of inherent conflicts of interest, but it first wishes to hear and assess Google's proposal," the EC added. 

  • Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) CEO Cristiano Amon said that Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) needs to improve its production technology in order for his company to consider using it as a supplier.

“Intel is not an option today,” Amon told Bloomberg TV on Friday. “We would like Intel to be an option.”

Qualcomm currently uses Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Samsung Electronics as suppliers.

data.title: Imperial Equities appoints Adam Chadi as COO
data.description:
• Imperial Equities (TSXV:IEI:CA) announced on Friday that it has appointed Adam Chadi, as the company's chief operating officer, effective September 1, 2025.
  • "I am pleased to welcome Adam to the senior leadership team of our growing company," said Sine Chadi, CEO. "With our strong investment portfolio and our ambitious plans for future growth, Adam's legal background and operational knowledge are poised to help us plan for the future focus."  
  • IEI:CA is +5.47% to $3.85
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crown Holdings upgraded to Buy at Truist as recent selloff presents attractive entry point
data.description:
Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK) +1.4% in Friday's trading as Truist Securities upgraded the maker of metal beverage and food cans to Buy from Hold with a $118 price target, citing the stock’s attractive entry point due to recent notable weakness and North America volume growth prospects in 2026.

Crown Holdings (NYSE:CCK) shares have declined nearly 8% since August 28 despite a lack of any specific actionable company news, and the stock now trades at ~8.1x 2026 EBITDA compared to a 9.5x average over the last five years, which Truist analyst Michael Roxland said presents an investment opportunity.

Despite recent modest volume softness, Roxland believes Crown (CCK) will meet its Q3 earnings guidance of $1.95-$2.05/share, partly due to strong operations and productivity, and the company also should be able to meet or exceed its full-year EPS outlook of $7.10-7.50, partly as it only raised guidance by $0.50 in Q2 while beating its own guidance for the first two quarters by $0.72 combined, implying continued conservatism.

For 2026, the analyst thinks Crown (CCK) could benefit from some new business wins in North
America, potentially leading to above market growth and ultimately contributing to share price outperformance.

data.title: RFK Jr. to suggest Tylenol use during pregnancy linked to autism - report
data.description:
An eagerly awaited report from HHS that was ordered by Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. will posit that the use of the pain killer Tylenol (acetaminophen) during pregnancy is linked to the development of autism in children

The document will also suggest that a lack of folate, also known as vitamin B9, can also contribute to autism.

The report will also argue that taking folinic acid, also known as leucovorin, can treat autism symptoms, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing sources.

Whether or not the report will delve into the role of vaccines in autism is uncertain. While the idea that vaccines can cause autism has been widely debunked, many vaccine skeptics still support the argument.

The report's drafting is being spearheaded by the NIH. NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya, FDA Commissioner Marty Makary, and CMS Director Mehmet Oz are said to have had a hand in the report. 

Sources told the newspaper that while the report is based on existing research, it will also cover other possible autism causes and offer additional areas for further study.

Tylenol is the brand name of acetaminophen sold by McNeil Consumer Healthcare, a subsidiary of Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE). A Kenvue spokesman told the Journal, "We have continuously evaluated the science and continue to believe there is no causal link between acetaminophen use during pregnancy and autism."

Kenvue is down ~13% in Friday afternoon trading.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Wall Street's major averages slide after briefly tagging record highs as payrolls data lands
data.description:
Wall Street’s broader market averages pare back earlier gains on Friday after all three major market averages breifly notched record highs all while yields slump as the jobs report came in cooler than expected.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) trades lower by 0.2%. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is in the red by 0.5%, while the blue-chip Dow (DJI) has slid 0.5%.

From a sector-by-sector perspective, four of the 11 S&P segments are trading in positive territory, led by Real Estate. At the other end of the spectrum, Energy has lagged the most so far.

In the bond market, yields have dropped as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) dipped 7 basis points to 4.08%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed down 8 basis points to 3.50%. Both the US2Y and US10Y have tagged 5-month lows.

On the economic front, August’s nonfarm payrolls report showed the economy added only 22,000 jobs—well below the 75,000 consensus forecast and trailing July’s 79,000 increase. At the same time, the unemployment rate ticked up to 4.3% from 4.2% in July, matching expectations.

"Friday's jobs report showed that employers are still hesitant to hire and is further confirmation that the labor market is weakening. This is the last jobs report before the Federal Reserve's September meeting and its weakness increases the chances that the Fed will proceed with its rate cut later this month," Rich Mullen, CEO, Pallas Capital Advisors stated.

Traders are now fully pricing in a rate cut in September after the latest labor report came in cooler than expected.

As for stocks that are on the move, Broadcom (AVGO) is up 9.6% after fiscal third quarter results beat estimates, and the company revealed a new customer, which saw bullish reactions from analysts.

data.title: Roblox unveils a new TikTok-like video feed for users
data.description:
Online gaming platform Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) announced the beta launch of a new on-platform short-form video experience for users 13 and older.

The company said Roblox Moments is a new way for users to discover experiences through short-form video clips of gameplay moments. Users can capture, edit, and share clips of their gameplay, while others can tap "join" to instantly try out the experience themselves. "We believe that the time people spend together on Roblox, the epic victories, the hilarious fails, and the unforgettable moments, deserve to be shared," highlighted the platform. Industry watchers called the new video feed a TikTok-like add-on for users.

Looking ahead, a set of APIs will be released soon to help Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) creators build their own in-experience content creation, sharing, and discovery systems. The tools were designed to support a new era of creativity, social interaction, and discovery on Roblox (RBLX), as well as unlock new opportunities for growth, monetization, and brand integrations.

The announcement arrived as part of a series of reveals from the Roblox Developers Conference.

Shares of Roblox (RBLX) traded flat on Friday. The stock is up more than 120% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: DMG Blockchain Solutions mines 23 Bitcoin in August
data.description:
• DMG Blockchain Solutions (OTCQB:DMGGF) announced on Friday that it mined 23 bitcoin in August, down from 26 in July, and it's hashrate also decreased to 1.53 EH/s from 1.65 EH/s a month ago. 
  • At the end of August, the company held 324 BTC, compared to 307 BTC a month ago.
  • DMGGF is +8.67% to $0.2004
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Quanex updates 2025 guidance to $1.82B revenue and $235M adjusted EBITDA amid integration progress and operational challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Quanex Building Products Corporation (NX) Q3 2025

Management View

  • George Wilson, President, CEO & Chairman, emphasized the company's resilience despite macro headwinds, noting "new operating segments are in place, synergy realization remains compelling and the cash flow generation of the combined entity has been strong." Wilson highlighted the integration of Tyman and the realization of cost synergies, stating, "After factoring in these additional synergies, mainly related to headcount, adjusting for lower volumes and pushing out the timing of when we should realize procurement savings, we still see a path to realizing approximately $45 million in cost synergies related to the Tyman acquisition over time."
  • Wilson detailed operational challenges at the window and door hardware business in Monterrey, Mexico, which "negatively impacted EBITDA in the Hardware Solutions segment by almost $5 million in the third quarter alone." He added, "We made leadership changes and are dedicating additional resources and capital to the facility to address and resolve these issues in an expedited manner."
  • Wilson reported a noncash goodwill impairment following the resegmentation of business units, clarifying "this impairment is not related to any performance indicators or changes to the long-term profitability expectations for our business."
  • CFO Scott Zuehlke stated, "On a consolidated basis, we reported net sales of $495.3 million during the third quarter of 2025, which represents an increase of approximately 77% compared to $280.3 million for the same period of 2024." Zuehlke attributed the increase mainly to the Tyman acquisition and discussed the $302.3 million noncash goodwill impairment, relating it to a lower market-based valuation due to share price at the trigger date.

Outlook

  • The company updated its fiscal 2025 guidance, now estimating "net sales of approximately $1.82 billion, which we expect will yield adjusted EBITDA of approximately $235 million" (Scott Zuehlke).
  • Zuehlke provided additional modeling assumptions for the full year 2025, including a gross margin of approximately 27%, SG&A of approximately $264 million, adjusted D&A of approximately $58 million, interest expense of approximately $53 million, an adjusted tax rate of 24.5%, CapEx of approximately $75 million and free cash flow of approximately $80 million.
  • Wilson indicated "volumes are expected to remain soft through the end of the year," but expressed confidence in a recovery when interest rates drop and consumer confidence improves.

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q3 2025 were $495.3 million, with adjusted EBITDA at $70.3 million, up 67.2% year-over-year, both driven by the Tyman acquisition and cost synergies.
  • Adjusted net income for Q3 2025 was $31.6 million or $0.69 per diluted share, compared to $26.9 million or $0.81 per diluted share in Q3 2024.
  • Hardware Solutions segment generated $227.1 million in net sales, with adjusted EBITDA of $24.7 million, impacted by the Monterrey facility issues.
  • Extruded Solutions segment reported $174.4 million in revenue and adjusted EBITDA of $37.1 million.
  • Custom Solutions segment had $102.3 million in net sales and $12.9 million in adjusted EBITDA.
  • Free cash flow for Q3 2025 was $46.2 million, and $51.25 million of bank debt was repaid.
  • The company repurchased 100,000 shares for approximately $2.1 million, with $33.6 million remaining under the repurchase program.

Q&A

  • Steven Ramsey, Thompson Research Group, LLC, asked about competitive landscape changes and Mexico headwinds. George Wilson responded that "the softness that we see is more specifically related to the softness in both R&R and new construction," and confirmed no destocking was observed.
  • Ramsey further asked about Tyman Mexico's EBITDA headwind. Zuehlke noted, "We do expect an impact in the fourth quarter. It may be similar to 3Q, depending on the progress that we show during the quarter."
  • Reuben Garner, The Benchmark Company, LLC, inquired about the balance of lower-than-expected results. Zuehlke cited "split between market and then procurement synergies specifically" as key factors.
  • Adam Thalhimer, Thompson, Davis & Company, Inc., asked about Q4 top line drivers and cash flow priorities. Zuehlke replied, "continuing to strengthen our balance sheet in this environment is a top priority."
  • Julio Romero, Sidoti & Company, LLC, sought details on Monterrey operational issues and Tyman integration. Wilson described the tooling and equipment problems and emphasized the mix of manual assembly and injection molding at Monterrey, with investments underway to resolve issues.
  • Romero also asked about the timeline for the first tranche of synergies. Zuehlke replied, "early 2026. I think that's still pretty accurate."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions focused on demand weakness, operational issues in Mexico, and guidance adjustments, displaying a slightly negative to neutral tone as they pressed for clarity on remediation and recovery timelines.
  • Management maintained a confident stance during prepared remarks, repeatedly emphasizing operational progress, cost synergy realization, and a strong balance sheet. In the Q&A, management's tone was more cautious, acknowledging challenges while reiterating focus on recovery and customer service, especially regarding Monterrey.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from highly optimistic about integration progress and synergy capture to a more defensive posture as they addressed operational setbacks and revised guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a downward revision in full-year guidance to $1.82 billion in net sales and $235 million in adjusted EBITDA, compared to the prior range of $1.84–$1.86 billion and $270–$280 million, reflecting softer demand and operational challenges.
  • The strategic focus has shifted toward resolving the Monterrey facility's issues and maintaining cost synergy realization, compared to the prior quarter's emphasis on integration progress and margin expansion.
  • Analysts in the current quarter focused more heavily on the impact and resolution timeline of operational disruptions, while previous quarter questions centered on integration synergies and tariff mitigation opportunities.
  • Management's overall confidence remains, but the language around short-term headwinds and recovery timelines is more measured than in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management explicitly pointed to macroeconomic headwinds, including soft consumer confidence, delayed construction activity, and tariff uncertainties in both North America and Europe.
  • The Monterrey, Mexico, facility's tooling and equipment issues have created inefficiencies, higher costs, and a significant EBITDA impact, with continued pressure expected in Q4.
  • Management has made leadership changes and committed additional resources and capital to address the Monterrey challenges, with expectations for tangible benefits in early fiscal 2026.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the pace of recovery, synergy realization, and the potential impact of industry consolidation among OEM customers.

Final Takeaway

Quanex’s third quarter was defined by successful integration progress, strong synergy capture, and robust cash flow, but was also tempered by operational setbacks in the Monterrey hardware business and persistent macroeconomic headwinds. Management emphasized confidence in the company’s long-term positioning, a clear path to $45 million in cost synergies, and the expectation that operational improvements and demand recovery will drive growth as the macro environment stabilizes.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
